Title: Hexokinase is necessary for glucose-mediated photosynthesis repression and lipid accumulation in a green alga

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Necessary for glucose-mediated repression of photosynthesis and for glucose-induced accumulation of astaxanthin, triacylglycerols, and cytoplasmic lipid droplets
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Astaxanthin is a high-value antioxidant; TAG is a biofuel precursor and storage lipid
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Cytoplasmic lipid droplets near the plasma membrane for neutral lipids/TAG; astaxanthin accumulates mainly as esterified mono- and diesters
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Rapidly upregulated by glucose within 30 min in wild type
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Rapidly upregulated 0.5 h after glucose addition in wild type; absent/attenuated in hxk1 mutants
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Glucose addition in the light (10 mM for qRT-PCR; 35 mM for physiology); 2-deoxy-D-glucose selection; 84 h treatment for phenotyping
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Forward genetic screen with 2-DOG resistance; whole-genome sequencing of eight independent mutants; Sanger confirmation; physiological assays (Fv/Fm, oxygen evolution/consumption); immunoblotting; HPLC pigment profiling; BODIPY-SIM imaging; TLC lipid analysis; qRT-PCR
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome sequencing of mutants, NCBI SRA primary accession SUB5868024; prior RNA-seq used to select responsive genes
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of CzHXK1 abolishes glucose-triggered astaxanthin and TAG accumulation and prevents photosynthetic shutdown; mutants still increase cell volume and respiration with glucose
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A key target to reprogram carbon partitioning in algae for biofuel precursor and astaxanthin production; manipulation could control trophic switching and lipid/carotenoid accumulation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Eight independent 2-DOG-resistant mutants all carried disruptive mutations in the single-copy CzHXK1 gene. 2. CzHXK1 is required for glucose-induced shutdown of photosynthesis and loss of photosynthetic apparatus. 3. CzHXK1 is required for high accumulation of astaxanthin, TAG, and cytoplasmic lipid droplets under glucose treatment. 4. CzHXK1 is required for normal glucose-responsive transcriptional repression of photosynthetic genes and activation of lipid/sugar metabolism genes.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although HXK1 is a glycolytic enzyme and proposed glucose sensor, the study could not distinguish whether its effect is due to signaling, catalysis, or both. The authors note a glucokinase gene (Cz06g03010) likely supports glucose metabolism in the dark in hxk1 mutants.
